title: pogostemon glaber var. glaber
intro: Pogostemon glaber var. glaber, also known as patchouli, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of patchouli include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies and aromatherapy products. Patchouli can be consumed as a tea, taken as a supplement, or used in topical preparations such as creams and ointments, and is also used in the production of perfumes and fragrances.
